title: Conditional Rendering with JSX
description: Render different UI based on conditions.

slides.title: if Statements Don't Work Inside JSX ❌
slides.content:
# Use Ternary or &&

Because you can't write `if` inside curly braces, you have two main options:

1. **Ternary operator** – for if/else
2. **Logical &&** – for rendering or nothing

You can also move the logic outside the JSX and store the result in a variable.

slides.title: Ternary Example
slides.content: Conditionally render one of two elements:

slides.title: && Example
slides.content: Render something only if a condition is true:

quizData.question:
What will the following JSX render if `unreadCount` is 0? ```jsx
{unreadCount &&

You have messages!

}
```
quizData.explanation: When `unreadCount` is 0, the expression evaluates to `0`, which React renders. To avoid that, use `unreadCount > 0 && ...`.
